prompt:
You are auditing configuration changes for Quasar Gateway across its 4.x
releases. You have been given eight documents (prepended above). The official
changelog, the migration guide, the configuration reference, and the
operations runbook are AUTHORITATIVE. The community wiki, the release blog, the
support KB, and the forum thread are SECONDARY and may be outdated, rounded for
marketing, or simply wrong. Where a secondary source disagrees with an
authoritative source, the authoritative source wins.

Determine each of the following and output them EXACTLY in the order and format
shown. For version numbers use the MAJOR.MINOR form (e.g. 4.3, not 4.3.0).
Report values as of the latest release unless a field says otherwise.

  POOL_MAXCONNECTIONS_DEFAULT: the correct current default of
    `pool.maxConnections` (an integer).
  TIMEOUT_READMS_DEFAULT: the correct current default of `timeout.readMs`
    (an integer, in ms).
  RETRY_MAXATTEMPTS_DEPRECATED_IN: the version in which `retry.maxAttempts`
    was deprecated.
  RETRY_MAXATTEMPTS_REMOVED_IN: the version in which `retry.maxAttempts` was
    removed entirely.
  LEGACYAUTHFILTER_REMOVED_IN: the version in which `LegacyAuthFilter` was
    removed.
  TLS_MINVERSION_RENAMED_TO: the new name of the key formerly called
    `tls.minVersion`.
  RENAMED_KEYS_COUNT: how many configuration keys are RENAMED across 4.x per
    the migration guide's own categorisation (renames only - not deprecations,
    replacements, or removals).
  WIKI_POOL_DEFAULT_CLAIM: the (possibly incorrect) value the COMMUNITY WIKI
    states for the `pool.maxConnections` default - i.e. report what the wiki
    says, to show you can attribute a claim to its source.

Output ONLY the eight lines, each formatted exactly as `FIELD: value`, between
<answer> and </answer> tags. No commentary, no blank lines, no extra text.

documents.title: Community Wiki - Quasar Upgrade Notes
documents.content:
# Community Wiki - Quasar Upgrade Notes (community-maintained)

> This page is edited by users and is OCCASIONALLY OUT OF DATE. Always verify
> against the official changelog and configuration reference before a
> production upgrade. Several sections below were last touched months ago.

## Overview
Quasar 4.x has been a mostly smooth line to run in production. This page
collects the gotchas the community has hit. Treat numbers here as folklore
unless you have checked them against the official docs.

## Connection pool
The default `pool.maxConnections` is 128 on current 4.x releases. (An editor
bumped this up from the very old value a while back; the precise figure has
been argued about in the forum thread, but 128 is the number most people
quote when someone asks in chat.) If you run hot, set it explicitly rather
than trusting the default.

## Retry configuration
`retry.maxAttempts` was removed in 4.4 - move to `retry.budget`. When we update
our upgrade playbooks we just treat `retry.maxAttempts -> retry.budget` as a
straight rename to keep the diff small; it has worked fine for us. Set the
budget to whatever your old max attempts was.

## TLS
Set `tls.minimumVersion`. Older configs and some of our internal templates
still spell it `tls.minVersion`; that spelling still works for now.

## Auth
If you were on the old LegacyAuthFilter you have to move to `auth.chain`. Give
yourself time; it is not a mechanical change. This bit us on the 4.3 upgrade.

## Read timeouts
Bump `timeout.readMs` if your upstreams are slow. The shipped default is fine
for most people so we have not written down what it currently is.

## Misc folklore
- Someone said the connection-reaper race was fixed "around 4.3"; sounds right.
- `cache.ttlSeconds` defaults to five minutes, we think.
- There is a `ratelimit.*` family now; off by default.

## Rate limiting
The `ratelimit.*` family arrived somewhere in the 4.2 era. It is off by default.
We turned it on with a per-route override and it worked fine. Someone claimed
the default rps is 100 but that does not match what we saw (it was disabled
until we set it), so take that with salt - check the config reference.

## Caching
`cache.ttlSeconds` exists; we run ours much higher than the default. We never
wrote down the exact default here; the config reference has it.

## Observability
There is head-based trace sampling now (`observability.traceSampleRate`). We
think it defaults to something small like a few percent. We set ours to 1.0 in
staging and a small value in prod.

## Common upgrade mistakes (community-reported)
- Forgetting to migrate off LegacyAuthFilter before 4.3. This is the big one.
- Leaving `retry.maxAttempts` in the config into the release that removes it and
  getting a boot failure. (People disagree about exactly which release removes
  it - see the retry section above and, honestly, just check the changelog.)
- Assuming the pool default is still 100. It was raised at some point; the wiki
  says 128 but a couple of people insist it is higher. We have not reconciled
  this; the official config reference is the tiebreaker, not this page.
- Spelling TLS the old way (`tls.minVersion`) forever. It still works but you
  should move to the new spelling.

## Version support (folklore, verify!)
- 4.0 and 4.1 are considered EOL by most teams now.
- 4.2 is the last release where retry.maxAttempts "just warns".
- 4.5 is current at time of writing. 4.5.1 is a small patch on top.
None of these support statements are authoritative; they are what people in the
channel say. For anything load-bearing, read the official changelog.

documents.title: Quasar Gateway - Official Release Changelog (4.x)
documents.content:
# Quasar Gateway - Official Release Changelog (4.x line)

This is the authoritative, engineering-maintained changelog for Quasar
Gateway. It is generated from merged release notes and reviewed by the release
manager for each tag. Where any community wiki, marketing blog, or support
article disagrees with this file, THIS FILE is correct. Entries are newest
first. Dates are the GA (general availability) dates in UTC.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 4.5.1 - 2026-07-14 (patch)
- Fixed a regression where `observability.traceSampleRate` was ignored when
  set to exactly 0. No configuration defaults changed in this patch.
- Fixed a rare panic in the `auth.chain` evaluator on empty header sets.

## 4.5.0 - 2026-06-30 (minor)
- BREAKING: the `retry.maxAttempts` key, which had been DEPRECATED since
  4.2.0, is now REMOVED. A gateway configuration that still sets
  `retry.maxAttempts` will refuse to start, emitting error QG-ERR-0091 and a
  pointer to `retry.budget`. This completes the retry-subsystem migration
  begun in 4.2.
- The default value of `retry.budget` is now 2. Prior to 4.5 the key existed
  but had no default (it had to be set explicitly, or retries were disabled).
- Added `observability.traceSampleRate` (default 0.05) for head-based trace
  sampling.
- Added `auth.chain.shortCircuitOnDeny` (default true).
- Deprecated `logging.legacyJsonShape` (to be removed in 5.0); no behaviour
  change yet.

## 4.4.1 - 2026-06-02 (patch)
- Fixed `pool.acquireTimeoutMs` not being honoured on the admin listener.
- Documentation-only correction to the TLS section (see 4.4.0). No defaults
  changed.

## 4.4.0 - 2026-05-20 (minor)
- CHANGED: the default value of `timeout.readMs` is raised from 3000 to 5000.
  Rationale: field data showed the 3000 default was too aggressive for
  cross-region upstreams. Deployments that pinned `timeout.readMs` explicitly
  are UNAFFECTED - only the default moves.
- RENAMED: the TLS floor key `tls.minVersion` is renamed to
  `tls.minimumVersion`. The old spelling continues to be accepted with a
  deprecation warning and will be removed in 5.0.0. This is a pure rename:
  behaviour is identical, only the key name changed.
- Added `pool.acquireTimeoutMs` (default 1000) bounding how long a request
  waits for a pooled upstream connection.
- Added `health.readyPath` (default "/readyz").
- Performance: 9% lower allocation on the response path.

## 4.3.2 - 2026-04-06 (patch)
- Security: fixed a header-smuggling issue in the legacy request parser
  (QG-CVE-2026-3). Backported to 4.2.4.

## 4.3.1 - 2026-03-23 (patch)
- Fixed a bug where `pool.maxConnections` set explicitly to 0 disabled
  pooling entirely instead of being rejected; 0 is now a validation error.
  NOTE: this patch does not change the DEFAULT of `pool.maxConnections`; the
  default remains as set in 4.3.0.
- Fixed slow shutdown when the connection reaper was mid-sweep.

## 4.3.0 - 2026-03-09 (minor)
- CHANGED: the default value of `pool.maxConnections` is raised from 100 to
  256. Rationale: better utilisation on multi-core proxy hosts. Explicitly
  configured values are unchanged; only the default moves.
- REMOVED: `LegacyAuthFilter` and all of its `auth.legacyFilter.*` keys are
  removed. Configurations using them must migrate to the `auth.chain`
  pipeline before upgrading to 4.3 (see the migration guide). A config still
  referencing `auth.legacyFilter.*` will fail validation at boot.
- Fixed a race in the connection reaper that could double-close a socket
  (internally tracked as QG-1042).
- Added `cache.ttlSeconds` (default 300).

## 4.2.4 - 2026-02-18 (patch)
- Security backport of the header-smuggling fix (see 4.3.2).

## 4.2.0 - 2026-01-12 (minor)
- DEPRECATED: `retry.maxAttempts` is deprecated in favour of `retry.budget`.
  For releases 4.2 through 4.4 it CONTINUES TO WORK but logs a deprecation
  warning on every boot. It is not removed until 4.5.0.
- Added `retry.budget`, a token-bucket retry budget. In 4.2 the key exists but
  has no default (retries stay disabled unless you set it).
- Added structured access logs (`logging.accessFormat`, default "json").
- Added `ratelimit.enabled` (default false) and `ratelimit.rps` (default 0).

## 4.1.0 - 2025-11-03 (minor)
- Added `timeout.readMs`, the upstream read timeout, with an initial default
  of 3000. (This default is later raised to 5000 in 4.4.0.)
- Added `timeout.connectMs` (default 1000).
- Performance: 12% lower p99 on the hot path.

## 4.0.0 - 2025-09-15 (major)
- RENAMED: the legacy `maxConns` key is renamed to `pool.maxConnections`. The
  default is unchanged at this release and remains 100 at 4.0. (The default is
  later raised to 256 in 4.3.0.)
- Removed the entire 3.x plugin ABI; plugins must be rebuilt against 4.x.
- Added the `auth.chain` pipeline (the eventual replacement for
  LegacyAuthFilter, which still ships in 4.0-4.2 and is removed in 4.3).
- Numerous internal refactors; see the 3.x -> 4.x migration guide.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Appendix A: configuration-change index
This index is generated from the entries above; it lists every key whose
DEFAULT changed during 4.x, the old and new value, and the release that made
the change. Keys not listed here kept their original default throughout 4.x.
  - `pool.maxConnections`:  100 -> 256   (changed in 4.3.0)
  - `timeout.readMs`:       3000 -> 5000 (changed in 4.4.0)
  - `retry.budget`:         (none) -> 2  (default introduced in 4.5.0)
Removed keys (no longer valid in the latest release):
  - `retry.maxAttempts`     (deprecated 4.2.0, removed 4.5.0)
  - `auth.legacyFilter.*`   (removed 4.3.0 with LegacyAuthFilter)
Renamed keys (old spelling -> new spelling):
  - `maxConns` -> `pool.maxConnections`      (4.0.0)
  - `tls.minVersion` -> `tls.minimumVersion` (4.4.0)

## Appendix B: unchanged defaults (for reference)
The following keys were added during 4.x and have NOT had their default
changed since introduction; do not confuse them with the keys in Appendix A:
  - `timeout.connectMs` = 1000 (since 4.1.0)
  - `pool.acquireTimeoutMs` = 1000 (since 4.4.0)
  - `cache.ttlSeconds` = 300 (since 4.3.0)
  - `ratelimit.enabled` = false (since 4.2.0)
  - `ratelimit.rps` = 0 (since 4.2.0)
  - `logging.accessFormat` = "json" (since 4.2.0)
  - `health.readyPath` = "/readyz" (since 4.4.0)
  - `observability.traceSampleRate` = 0.05 (since 4.5.0)
  - `tls.minimumVersion` = "1.2" (value unchanged; only the KEY name changed
    in 4.4.0)

## Appendix C: error-code reference (selected)
- QG-ERR-0091: a removed key is present in the config (currently only
  `retry.maxAttempts` triggers this, as of 4.5.0).
- QG-ERR-0044: `pool.maxConnections` set to 0 (rejected since 4.3.1).
- QG-ERR-0102: `auth.legacyFilter.*` present (removed since 4.3.0).
- QG-WARN-0210: deprecated key present but still honoured (emitted for
  `retry.maxAttempts` on 4.2-4.4, and for `tls.minVersion` on 4.4+).

## Appendix D: per-release upgrade checklist
4.0: rename `maxConns` -> `pool.maxConnections`; rebuild all plugins against the
  4.x ABI; the pool default is 100 at this release.
4.1: optionally adopt `timeout.readMs` (introduced at 3000) and
  `timeout.connectMs`; no removals.
4.2: `retry.maxAttempts` starts warning (QG-WARN-0210); introduce `retry.budget`
  (still no default); `ratelimit.*` available but off.
4.3: BEFORE upgrading, migrate off `LegacyAuthFilter` to `auth.chain` (4.3
  removes it, QG-ERR-0102 if left); after upgrading, note the pool default is
  now 256; `cache.ttlSeconds` (300) is available.
4.4: rename `tls.minVersion` -> `tls.minimumVersion` (old name warns); note the
  read-timeout default is now 5000; `pool.acquireTimeoutMs` (1000) available.
4.5: remove `retry.maxAttempts` from the config BEFORE upgrading (4.5 rejects
  it, QG-ERR-0091); set `retry.budget` deliberately (new default 2);
  `observability.traceSampleRate` (0.05) available.

## Appendix E: provenance
This changelog is generated from signed release tags. The migration guide and
the configuration reference are generated from the same source of truth and
are guaranteed consistent with the entries above. Community pages (wiki, forum),
marketing posts (release blog), and older support KB articles are NOT part of
this pipeline and can lag or misstate; when they disagree with this file, this
file governs.

documents.title: Release Blog - What's New in Quasar 4.3
documents.content:
# What's New in Quasar 4.3 (official release blog / marketing)

Quasar 4.3 is our biggest performance-focused release of the year, and we are
thrilled to get it into your hands. Here are the highlights.

## We DOUBLED the default connection pool
The headline change: we doubled the default connection pool. The new default is
250 connections, up from 100 - so you get dramatically more headroom out of the
box, with no config changes required. Benchmarks on multi-core proxy hosts show
big throughput gains just from the new default.

(For exact, authoritative numbers always see the changelog and the
configuration reference; blog posts round for readability.)

## Goodbye LegacyAuthFilter, hello auth.chain
We have retired the old LegacyAuthFilter in favour of the composable
`auth.chain` pipeline. If you are still on LegacyAuthFilter, plan your
migration before upgrading to 4.3, because 4.3 removes it.

## Reliability fixes
We squashed a nasty connection-reaper race that a few large customers had hit
under churn. Upgrade for a calmer on-call rotation.

## Also in 4.3
- A new response caching layer (`cache.ttlSeconds`) for teams that can tolerate
  short staleness on hot paths.
- Faster startup and a calmer connection reaper.

## A note on numbers in this post
Marketing posts round for readability. The "250" above is a rounded, headline
figure for the new pool default; the precise shipped value and every other
exact default live in the changelog and the configuration reference, which are
the sources of truth. If you are automating an upgrade, script against those,
not against this blog.

## What's next
Look out for read-timeout default tuning and a TLS configuration cleanup in an
upcoming release (spoiler: the read-timeout default goes up, and the TLS
minimum-version key gets a cleaner name). As always, the migration guide has
the details. Onward!

documents.title: Quasar Gateway 3.x -> 4.x Migration Guide
documents.content:
# Quasar Gateway 3.x -> 4.x Migration Guide (authoritative)

Read this alongside the official changelog. This guide's job is to categorise
every configuration change so that upgrade playbooks and automation stay
correct. The single most important distinction it draws - and the one teams
most often get wrong - is between a RENAME and a DEPRECATION/REPLACEMENT. Those
are tracked in two separate sections below and must not be conflated, because
a rename is behaviour-preserving whereas a replacement is not.

## 1. Renamed configuration keys
A rename changes only the spelling of a key; the behaviour, type, and default
are unchanged (unless a separate changelog entry also changes the default).
Across the ENTIRE 4.x line there are EXACTLY TWO renamed keys:
  1. `maxConns` -> `pool.maxConnections`      (renamed in 4.0.0)
  2. `tls.minVersion` -> `tls.minimumVersion` (renamed in 4.4.0)
That is the complete list. If your automation rewrites key names on upgrade,
these are the only two rewrites it should perform. Nothing else in 4.x is a
rename.

## 2. Deprecated / replaced / removed keys (these are NOT renames)
Each item here changes behaviour, is superseded by a differently-behaving key,
or is removed. They are deliberately excluded from the rename list in section 1:
  - `retry.maxAttempts`: DEPRECATED in 4.2.0, REMOVED in 4.5.0. It is replaced
    by `retry.budget`. Crucially, `retry.budget` is a TOKEN-BUCKET budget, not
    a fixed attempt count, so a mechanical `retry.maxAttempts -> retry.budget`
    substitution is WRONG - the semantics differ and you must re-think the
    value. Because the behaviour differs, this is a replacement, NOT a rename;
    do not count it among the renamed keys in section 1.
  - `LegacyAuthFilter` (and its `auth.legacyFilter.*` sub-keys): REMOVED in
    4.3.0, replaced by the `auth.chain` pipeline. This is a re-architecture,
    not a rename.
  - `logging.legacyJsonShape`: deprecated in 4.5.0, slated for removal in
    5.0.0. Still functional in 4.5.

## 3. Default-value changes you must review before upgrading
Defaults that moved during 4.x (review these even if you never set the key):
  - `pool.maxConnections`: default changed 100 -> 256 in 4.3.0.
  - `timeout.readMs`: default changed 3000 -> 5000 in 4.4.0.
  - `retry.budget`: gains a default of 2 in 4.5.0 (previously no default).
If you rely on the old default behaviour, PIN the value explicitly before you
upgrade past the release that changed it.

## 4. Recommended upgrade order (one minor at a time)
1. 3.x -> 4.0: rename `maxConns` -> `pool.maxConnections`; rebuild plugins.
2. 4.0 -> 4.1: adopt `timeout.readMs` / `timeout.connectMs` if you want them.
3. 4.1 -> 4.2: introduce `retry.budget`; note `retry.maxAttempts` now warns.
4. 4.2 -> 4.3: migrate off `LegacyAuthFilter` to `auth.chain` BEFORE upgrading
   (4.3 removes it); review the new `pool.maxConnections` default of 256.
5. 4.3 -> 4.4: rename `tls.minVersion` -> `tls.minimumVersion`; review the new
   `timeout.readMs` default of 5000.
6. 4.4 -> 4.5: remove `retry.maxAttempts` from your config (4.5 rejects it);
   set `retry.budget` deliberately (its new default is 2).

## 5. auth.chain migration (from LegacyAuthFilter)
LegacyAuthFilter applied a single monolithic filter. `auth.chain` is an ordered
list of stages, each of which may allow, deny, or annotate a request. A typical
one-to-one port maps the old filter's checks onto a two-stage chain (an
authentication stage followed by an authorization stage). See the reference
configuration for a worked example. Note that there is no `auth.legacyFilter.*`
compatibility shim in 4.3+, so the port must be complete before upgrading.

## 6. Frequently asked questions
Q: Is `retry.maxAttempts -> retry.budget` just a rename I can script?
A: No. It is listed in section 2, not section 1, precisely because it is not a
rename. Scripting a blind substitution will set a token-bucket budget to what
used to be an attempt count and change your retry behaviour.

Q: How many keys are actually renamed in 4.x?
A: Two. See section 1. Anything else you have heard described as a "rename"
(for example the retry change) is a replacement or removal, tracked in
section 2.

## 7. Validation before and after each hop
Run `quasar config validate --strict` against your config at every hop. In
strict mode the validator will:
  - reject a removed key (QG-ERR-0091 for `retry.maxAttempts` on 4.5+),
  - reject `auth.legacyFilter.*` on 4.3+ (QG-ERR-0102),
  - warn on a deprecated-but-honoured key (QG-WARN-0210),
  - print every default that differs between your pinned values and the
    target release's defaults, so you can see, for example, that
    `pool.maxConnections` moved to 256 or `timeout.readMs` moved to 5000 if
    you had been relying on the old defaults.
Treat any QG-ERR as a hard stop; treat QG-WARN as a follow-up task.

## 8. Rollback notes
Each 4.x minor is forward-only for its schema changes, but a rollback within a
minor line (e.g. 4.4.1 -> 4.4.0) is safe. Rolling BACK across the 4.5 boundary
requires re-adding `retry.maxAttempts` only if you had removed it AND you
depend on its old semantics; prefer keeping `retry.budget` set so a
roll-forward is clean. Rolling back across 4.3 requires restoring a
LegacyAuthFilter config, which most teams will not keep around - plan to go
forward, not back, past 4.3.

## 9. Per-environment guidance
- Staging: take each hop a full day ahead of production and watch the
  QG-WARN-0210 count trend to zero before proceeding.
- Production: pin the three defaults in Appendix A of the changelog
  (`pool.maxConnections`, `timeout.readMs`, `retry.budget`) explicitly during
  the migration window so that a default change never surprises you
  mid-upgrade; unpin later once you have chosen values deliberately.
- Canary: the config-sync system can target a single canary host, but be aware
  that a mis-scoped change set can be applied fleet-wide - always confirm the
  scope flag on pool-sizing and timeout changes.

## 10. Glossary
- RENAME: the key's spelling changes; behaviour, type, and default are
  preserved. Exactly two occur in 4.x (section 1).
- DEPRECATION: the key still works but warns; it is scheduled for removal.
- REMOVAL: the key is rejected; the gateway will not start if it is present.
- REPLACEMENT: a new, differently-behaving key supersedes an old one (e.g.
  `retry.budget` replaces `retry.maxAttempts`); NOT a rename.
- DEFAULT CHANGE: the key's spelling is unchanged but its out-of-the-box value
  moves (e.g. `pool.maxConnections` 100 -> 256); review even if you never set
  the key.

## 11. Automation and CI notes
If you rewrite configuration keys automatically during upgrades:
  - Perform ONLY the renames listed in section 1. Do NOT add a
    `retry.maxAttempts` -> `retry.budget` rewrite; that pair is a replacement
    with different semantics (section 2), and a blind rewrite will silently
    change retry behaviour. Deciding what belongs in your rewrite list requires
    reading section 1 versus section 2 carefully - the categories, not the
    surface similarity of the names, determine what is a rename.
  - Add a CI gate that runs `quasar config validate --strict` against the
    target release and fails the build on any QG-ERR. Treat QG-WARN-0210 as a
    tracked debt, not a blocker.
  - Snapshot the effective defaults of the target release and diff them against
    your pinned values, so any default change (see section 3 and the changelog's
    configuration-change index for which defaults moved and in which release)
    never lands silently.
  - Fail the build early if a key the target release has removed still appears
    in your config; consult section 2 for the removed keys and the release that
    removed each.

## 12. If in doubt
Do not rely on memory, chat, wikis, or blog posts for any of these facts. The
exact defaults, the deprecation and removal versions, and the rename list are
all in this guide, the changelog, and the configuration reference. Read those,
and where a secondary source contradicts them, the authoritative sources win.

documents.title: Forum thread - default for pool.maxConnections
documents.content:
# Forum thread: "What's the real default for pool.maxConnections in 4.x?"

user_dgrey: Quick sanity check - the wiki says pool.maxConnections defaults to
  128 now. Is that right? I could have sworn it used to be 100.

user_ito: It was 100 for ages. I *think* they bumped it in 4.3 but I don't
  remember the exact number. The wiki says 128 so probably that.

user_release_bot: Reminder: the wiki is community-edited. For authoritative
  defaults use the official changelog or the configuration reference, not this
  thread and not the wiki.

user_dgrey: Fair. Separately - did retry.maxAttempts get removed already? Ops
  swears it threw a startup error after our last upgrade.

user_ito: If you upgraded to 4.5, yes - it's gone in 4.5 and the gateway won't
  boot if it's set. Before that it just warned. Some folks think it went away
  back in 4.4 but I'm fairly sure the hard removal was 4.5.

user_dgrey: Thanks. I'll go read the actual changelog instead of trusting chat.

user_mchen: Late to this - we hit the retry.maxAttempts boot failure on our 4.5
  rollout last week. Definitely 4.5. The error was QG-ERR-0091. Before 4.5 it
  only logged a warning for us on 4.3 and 4.4.

user_ito: Good data point. So: deprecated in 4.2, warns through 4.4, hard-removed
  in 4.5. The "removed in 4.4" thing on the wiki is just wrong then.

user_dgrey: Confirmed against the changelog - deprecated 4.2.0, removed 4.5.0.
  I'll fix the wiki when I get a sec (though wikis being wrong is a law of
  nature).

user_kpatel: Separate question - what's the read timeout default these days? An
  old support KB says 3 seconds but that article is ancient.

user_ito: It was raised. The KB is stale. Config reference will have the current
  number; I don't trust my memory on the exact ms.

user_kpatel: Right, the KB even warns it predates a bunch of releases. Checking
  the reference. Thanks all.

user_release_bot: Reminder (again): the wiki, this forum, marketing blogs, and
  old KB articles are all SECONDARY. The changelog, the migration guide, and the
  configuration reference are the sources of truth for defaults, deprecations,
  and removals.

documents.title: Quasar Gateway - Configuration Reference (excerpt, 4.5)
documents.content:
# Quasar Gateway - Configuration Reference (excerpt, current as of 4.5)

Authoritative per-key reference generated from the 4.5 schema. Each entry lists
the key, type, current default, and the release that last changed the default.

## pool.*
- `pool.maxConnections` (int) - maximum pooled upstream connections. Current
  default: 256. (Default last changed in 4.3.0, from 100.) Must be > 0.
- `pool.acquireTimeoutMs` (int) - max wait to acquire a pooled connection.
  Current default: 1000. Added in 4.4.0.

## timeout.*
- `timeout.readMs` (int) - upstream read timeout in milliseconds. Current
  default: 5000. (Default last changed in 4.4.0, from 3000.) Added in 4.1.0.
- `timeout.connectMs` (int) - upstream connect timeout. Current default: 1000.

## retry.*
- `retry.budget` (float) - token-bucket retry budget. Current default: 2.
  Added in 4.2.0 (no default until 4.5.0). Replaced the removed
  `retry.maxAttempts`.
- `retry.maxAttempts` - REMOVED in 4.5.0 (deprecated 4.2.0). Setting it is now
  a fatal configuration error (QG-ERR-0091).

## tls.*
- `tls.minimumVersion` (string) - minimum negotiated TLS version. Formerly
  spelled `tls.minVersion` (renamed in 4.4.0). Current default: "1.2".

## auth.*
- `auth.chain` (list) - ordered authentication/authorization stages. Added in
  4.0.0; sole supported auth mechanism from 4.3.0 (LegacyAuthFilter removed).

## cache.* / ratelimit.* / observability.*
- `cache.ttlSeconds` (int) - default 300 (added 4.3.0).
- `ratelimit.enabled` (bool) - default false (added 4.2.0).
- `ratelimit.rps` (int) - default 0 (added 4.2.0).
- `observability.traceSampleRate` (float) - default 0.05 (added 4.5.0).

## server.* / listener.*
- `server.workerThreads` (int) - default 0 (0 = auto-size to CPU count).
- `server.maxHeaderBytes` (int) - default 16384.
- `server.gracefulShutdownMs` (int) - default 30000.
- `listener.http.port` (int) - default 8080.
- `listener.admin.port` (int) - default 9090.
- `listener.http.keepAliveMs` (int) - default 75000.

## logging.* / health.*
- `logging.accessFormat` (string) - default "json" (added 4.2.0).
- `logging.level` (string) - default "info".
- `logging.legacyJsonShape` (bool) - default false; deprecated in 4.5.0.
- `health.readyPath` (string) - default "/readyz" (added 4.4.0).
- `health.livePath` (string) - default "/livez".

## Notes on defaults
Only three keys changed their default during 4.x: `pool.maxConnections`
(-> 256 in 4.3.0), `timeout.readMs` (-> 5000 in 4.4.0), and `retry.budget`
(-> 2 in 4.5.0). Every other key in this reference has held its default since
the release that introduced it. When in doubt, this reference and the
changelog are authoritative; community pages are not.

documents.title: Support KB QG-1042 - Read Timeouts
documents.content:
# Support KB QG-1042 - Read Timeouts and the Connection Reaper

Applies to: Quasar Gateway 4.x. Last substantive edit predates several 4.x
releases; confirm current defaults in the changelog for YOUR version.

## Symptom
Intermittent upstream read timeouts under load, surfacing as 504s to callers.

## Background
On older installs, `timeout.readMs` defaults to 3s (3000 ms). If you are seeing
read timeouts, raising this value is often the first thing to try, especially
for cross-region upstreams. Because this article is older than several 4.x
releases, do not assume 3000 is still the default on a recent version - check
the changelog; the default has been revisited.

## The connection-reaper race
Some customers hit a race in the connection reaper that could double-close a
socket during a sweep. This was fixed in 4.3.0 (patch-tracked as QG-1042).
If you are below 4.3.0 and see sporadic connection resets during low-traffic
windows, upgrading is the fix.

## Related tuning
- `timeout.connectMs` governs the connect phase separately from reads.
- `pool.acquireTimeoutMs` (4.4+) bounds waiting for a pooled connection; if you
  see acquisition stalls rather than upstream timeouts, look here.

## Distinguishing read timeouts from pool-acquisition stalls
A frequent support theme: customers raise `timeout.readMs` when the real
problem is pool acquisition. The two look alike on a latency graph but differ
in the logs. If you see "upstream read timeout", the read phase is the problem
and `timeout.readMs` is the knob. If you see "acquire wait" or "pool at
capacity", the connection pool is the problem and `pool.maxConnections` /
`pool.acquireTimeoutMs` are the knobs - raising `timeout.readMs` will not help
and may mask the real issue. Always read the log line, not just the graph.

## Boot failures after upgrade
- QG-ERR-0091 at boot: a removed key is present. As of the current line this is
  `retry.maxAttempts`, which the latest release rejects. Remove it and set
  `retry.budget`.
- QG-ERR-0102 at boot: `auth.legacyFilter.*` present after LegacyAuthFilter was
  removed. Migrate to `auth.chain`.
- QG-ERR-0044 at boot: `pool.maxConnections` set to 0. Set a positive value.

## Escalation checklist
1. Capture the exact error class from the logs (read timeout vs acquire wait vs
   boot error code).
2. Confirm your running version and check the changelog for the CURRENT default
   of any key you are about to change - do not rely on this article's numbers,
   which predate several releases.
3. Note that a recent fleet-wide configuration change can alter a shared setting
   (for example a connection-pool size) out from under a single service; if
   multiple services degrade at once, suspect a shared change before a local
   one.

documents.title: Quasar Gateway 4.x - Operations Runbook (excerpt)
documents.content:
# Quasar Gateway 4.x - Operations Runbook (excerpt)

This runbook covers day-2 operations for Quasar Gateway 4.x fleets. It is a
PROCEDURAL document: for exact configuration defaults always consult the
official changelog or the configuration reference, not this runbook. Values
are intentionally not restated here so this page does not drift.

## 1. Health and readiness
Each gateway exposes a liveness endpoint and a readiness endpoint (see the
configuration reference for `health.livePath` and `health.readyPath`). Load
balancers should gate traffic on readiness, not liveness. A gateway reports
NOT ready while it drains connections during a graceful shutdown; the drain
window is governed by `server.gracefulShutdownMs`.

## 2. Rolling restarts
Restart one instance at a time, waiting for readiness to return before moving
to the next. During a restart, in-flight upstream calls are allowed to finish
up to the graceful-shutdown window. If you have raised `timeout.readMs` for
slow upstreams, ensure the graceful-shutdown window is at least as large, or
long reads will be cut off at shutdown.

## 3. Connection-pool operations
The upstream connection pool is sized by `pool.maxConnections` and its
acquisition wait is bounded by `pool.acquireTimeoutMs`. Symptoms of an
undersized pool are acquisition-timeout errors and requests queueing for a
connection; symptoms of upstream slowness are read timeouts. These look
similar on a latency graph but have different fixes - check which error class
dominates in the logs before changing the pool size. Never set the pool size
to 0 (rejected since 4.3.1 with QG-ERR-0044).

## 4. Retry behaviour
Retries are governed by `retry.budget` (a token-bucket budget). On 4.5+ the
old `retry.maxAttempts` key is rejected at boot; if a restart fails with
QG-ERR-0091, search the config for `retry.maxAttempts` and remove it, then set
`retry.budget` deliberately. Over-large budgets can amplify an upstream
incident into a retry storm - prefer a small budget with a circuit breaker.

## 5. TLS
The negotiated floor is `tls.minimumVersion` (older configs spell the same key
`tls.minVersion`; both are accepted for now, but standardise on the new
spelling). Rotating certificates does not require a restart if hot-reload is
enabled.

## 6. Common alerts and first responses
- Read-timeout rate up: check upstream health first; only then consider
  `timeout.readMs`.
- Acquisition-timeout rate up: check `pool.maxConnections` headroom and
  whether a recent config change altered it fleet-wide.
- Boot loop with QG-ERR-0091 or QG-ERR-0102: a removed key is present; consult
  the migration guide's section 2.
- QG-WARN-0210 climbing: a deprecated key is still in the config; schedule its
  removal before the next major.

## 7. Change management
Configuration is delivered by the config-sync system. Pool-sizing and timeout
changes MUST carry an explicit scope flag; a change intended for a canary that
is accidentally scoped fleet-wide can starve every service sharing a pool.
Review the scope on every pool/timeout change set before it rolls out.
